# Dependencies — Line 1c (Tip income not reported on line 1a) and Form 4137

This document covers every output produced by the line 1c / tip income computation pass.

| Output | Return field | Location |
|---|---|---|
| `form1040.income.tipIncome` | Form 1040 line 1c | Unreported tip income (cash, charge, allocated, noncash) |
| `taxAndCredits.form4137Taxpayer` | Form 4137 (taxpayer) | SS and Medicare tax on unreported tips |
| `taxAndCredits.form4137Spouse` | Form 4137 (spouse) | Same for spouse; MFJ only (skipped on MFS) |
| `schedule2.otherTaxes.unreportedTipIncomeTax` | Schedule 2 line 5 | Form 4137 line 13 aggregate across spouses |
| `form8959.partI.unreportedTipsMedicare` | Form 8959 Part I | Form 4137 line 6 feeds Additional Medicare Tax base |

Blocking and advisory flags emitted by this pass are listed at the end.

---

## Output 1: `form1040.income.tipIncome` — Form 1040 Line 1c

### Formula

```
Per person:
  line1c_person = Σ unreportedForIncome   (per-employer, see decision tree)
                + Σ nonCashTipsFmv        (per-employer)

Aggregated:
  Form1040.line1c = taxpayer.line1c + spouse.line1c   (spouse skipped on MFS)
```

### `unreportedForIncome` per-employer decision tree

```
unreportedCashCharge = max(0, totalTipsReceived − totalTipsReportedToEmployer)

if allocatedTipsW2Box8 > 0:
  if hasAdequateRecordsUnreportedLessThanAllocated == true
     AND substantiatedUnreportedTipsIfLessThanAllocated < allocatedTipsW2Box8:
       unreportedForIncome = substantiatedUnreportedTipsIfLessThanAllocated   ← adequate-records reduction
  else:
       unreportedForIncome = max(unreportedCashCharge, allocatedTipsW2Box8)   ← allocated-tips override
else:
       unreportedForIncome = unreportedCashCharge                              ← plain unreported
```

### Always-required inputs

| Input | Source form | Firestore path | Field | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Tip employer list (taxpayer) | `tip-income-taxpayer` personal form | `users/{uid}/tip-income-taxpayer` | `tipsByEmployer` (array) | If empty AND `hasUnreportedTips != false`, backend auto-builds entries from W-2s with `allocatedTipsW2Box8 > 0` matching taxpayer SSN |
| Tip employer list (spouse) | `tip-income-spouse` personal form | `users/{uid}/tip-income-spouse` | `tipsByEmployer` (array) | MFS guard since 2026-05-06 skips `computeTipsForPerson(spouse)` entirely on MFS |
| Filing status | `filing-status` personal form | `users/{uid}/filing-status` | `filingStatus` (string) | Used to derive `isMfs`; must be `"Married filing separately"` (display name) to trigger MFS guard |

### Conditional inputs

| Input | Condition | Source form | Field | Effect if absent |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Has unreported tips (taxpayer) | SOFT gate | `tip-income-taxpayer` | `hasUnreportedTips` (boolean) | Explicit `false` → skip computation entirely; `null` falls through to preserve W-2 box 8 auto-fill; `true` runs per-employer computation. UI's `normalizeForSave` clears `tipsByEmployer` when not `true` (defense in depth) |
| Has unreported tips (spouse) | Same | `tip-income-spouse` | `hasUnreportedTips` | Same gate; only read on non-MFS returns |

### Per-employer inputs (within `tipsByEmployer[]`)

| Field | Purpose | Effect if absent |
|---|---|---|
| `totalTipsReceived` | Form 4137 col (c): total cash & charge tips received | Treated as $0 |
| `totalTipsReportedToEmployer` | Form 4137 col (d): tips reported to employer | Treated as $0; unreported = received |
| `allocatedTipsW2Box8` | W-2 box 8 allocated tips; auto-filled from matching W-2 | Absent/zero → no allocated-tips override |
| `hasAdequateRecordsUnreportedLessThanAllocated` | Boolean; enables substantiated reduction | Absent/false → allocated-tips override applies |
| `substantiatedUnreportedTipsIfLessThanAllocated` | Actual unreported (< allocated) | Used only when `hasAdequateRecords == true` AND value < allocated. Missing while flag is true → `ADEQUATE_RECORDS_MISSING_SUBSTANTIATED_AMOUNT_*` blocking flag |
| `nonCashTipsFmv` | FMV of property tips (tickets, passes, merchandise) | Absent/zero → no noncash income added |
| `line5Under20PerMonthTips` | Tips under $20/month per employer — reduces Form 4137 line 6 ONLY (not line 1c) | Absent/zero → no reduction |
| `medicareOnlyGovernmentTips` | Govt employee tips: 1.45% Medicare only, not 6.2% SS | Absent/zero → no exclusion from SS base |
| `rRTACompensationW2Box14` | Railroad retirement comp; added to Form 4137 line 8 SS wage base | Absent/zero → no RRTA contribution |
| `employerName` | Display + Form 4137 employer table | Absent → empty string |
| `employerEIN` | Required when Form 4137 applies for this employer | Missing/blank when Form 4137 was built → `MISSING_EMPLOYER_EIN_FORM_4137_*` blocking flag |
| `socialSecurityWagesW2Box3` / `socialSecurityTipsW2Box7` | Fallback for Form 4137 line 8 | Used ONLY when no matching W-2 exists for the SSN |

### W-2 auto-fill source

| Input | Source | Firestore path | Field |
|---|---|---|---|
| W-2 statements for matching SSN | W-2 statement entries | `users/{uid}/w-2/{entryId}` | `employeeSSN`, `allocatedTipsW2Box8`, `employerName`, `employerEIN`, `socialSecurityWagesAmount` (box 3), `socialSecurityTipsAmount` (box 7) |

---

## Output 2 & 3: Form 4137 (Taxpayer and Spouse)

### Creation gate

```
Form 4137 is built only when:
  line4UnreportedTips > 0 AND (line6UnreportedTipsSubjectMedicare > 0 OR totalAllocatedTips > 0)
```

All-noncash or all-under-$20 scenarios produce NO Form 4137 (no FICA owed via this path).

### Line-by-line computations

| Form 4137 line | Computation | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Line 2 | `Σ totalTipsReceived` | |
| Line 3 | `Σ totalTipsReportedToEmployer` | |
| Line 4 | `Σ unreportedForIncome` | Per decision tree above |
| Line 5 | `Σ line5Under20PerMonthTips` | |
| Line 6 | `max(0, Line 4 − Line 5)` | Feeds Form 8959 Part I |
| Line 7 | `$176,100` | `ReferenceData.SOCIAL_SECURITY_WAGE_BASE` (2025) |
| Line 8 | `ssWagesTips + totalRrtaComp` | See SS wage-base sourcing below |
| Line 9 | `max(0, Line 7 − Line 8)` | |
| Line 10 | `min(Line 6 − medicareOnlyTips, Line 9)` | |
| Line 11 | `Line 10 × 6.2%` | Null when `MISSING_SS_W2_DATA_*` fires |
| Line 12 | `Line 6 × 1.45%` | Always computed |
| Line 13 | `Line 11 + Line 12` | Carries to Schedule 2 line 5 |

### SS wage-base sourcing for Form 4137 line 8 — W-2 is canonical

| Source | When used |
|---|---|
| `sumW2SocialSecurityWagesAndTipsForSsn(w2Entries, ssn)` (W-2 boxes 3+7, SSN-filtered) | **Primary** — used whenever non-null, INCLUDING `BigDecimal.ZERO`. If a matching W-2 has explicit $0 in boxes 3+7, fallback is silently ignored (typo'd W-2 must be corrected at source). Locked in by `line1cSsWageBaseW2IsCanonicalEvenWhenZero`. |
| Per-employer `socialSecurityWagesW2Box3` + `socialSecurityTipsW2Box7` on the tip-income form | **Fallback only** — used ONLY when no matching W-2 exists for the SSN |

---

## Output 4: `schedule2.otherTaxes.unreportedTipIncomeTax` — Schedule 2 Line 5

```
schedule2.unreportedTipIncomeTax = (form4137Taxpayer.line13 ?? 0) + (form4137Spouse.line13 ?? 0)
```

Set to null when neither form exists.

---

## Output 5: Form 8959 Integration — Additional Medicare Tax Base

`Form 4137 line 6` (unreported tips subject to Medicare) feeds Form 8959 Part I as part of total wages subject to the 0.9% Additional Medicare Tax:

```
form8959.partI.totalMedicareWages =
  Σ W-2 box 5 medicareWagesAndTipsAmount (SSN-filtered)
  + form4137Taxpayer.line6
  + form4137Spouse.line6
  + form8919Taxpayer.line6
  + form8919Spouse.line6

additionalMedicareTax = max(0, totalMedicareWages − threshold) × 0.009
threshold = $250,000 MFJ / $200,000 Single,HOH,QSS / $125,000 MFS
→ schedule2.otherTaxes.additionalMedicareTax
```

---

## Validation Flags

| Flag | Condition | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| `MISSING_SS_W2_DATA_TAXPAYER` | Form 4137 line 6 > 0 AND no W-2 SS data for taxpayer SSN AND form-input fallback also null | **Blocking**. Line 11 (SS tax) set to null; Medicare tax (line 12) still computed |
| `MISSING_SS_W2_DATA_SPOUSE` | Same for spouse | Blocking |
| `ADEQUATE_RECORDS_MISSING_SUBSTANTIATED_AMOUNT_TAXPAYER` | Any employer has `hasAdequateRecordsUnreportedLessThanAllocated == true` AND `substantiated == null` AND `allocatedTipsW2Box8 > 0` | **Blocking** (since 2026-05-19 per save-vs-compute principle). Compute uses allocated-tips fallback so Line 1c is too high; blocking flag prevents persistence. UI shows advisory hint but never refuses save |
| `ADEQUATE_RECORDS_MISSING_SUBSTANTIATED_AMOUNT_SPOUSE` | Same for spouse | Blocking |
| `MISSING_EMPLOYER_EIN_FORM_4137_TAXPAYER` | Form 4137 was built AND any employer row has missing/blank `employerEIN` | **Blocking** (since 2026-05-19). Compute math is correct; blocking flag prevents persistence of a Form 4137 that IRS e-file would reject |
| `MISSING_EMPLOYER_EIN_FORM_4137_SPOUSE` | Same for spouse | Blocking |

Non-flag defensive behaviors (silent skip):
- Negative unreported clamped to zero.
- Allocated-tips override forces minimum (per Pub 531).
- Form 4137 NOT created when only noncash tips (no FICA on noncash).
- Form 4137 NOT created when all under-$20 (no Medicare base remaining).

---

## Absence / Missing-data Behaviour

| Missing input | Backend behaviour |
|---|---|
| `tip-income-taxpayer` form not saved AND no W-2 allocated tips for SSN | Person contributes $0 to line 1c; no Form 4137 |
| `tip-income-taxpayer` form not saved AND W-2 has allocated tips for SSN | `buildTipEntriesFromW2()` creates synthetic entries; Form 4137 computed |
| `totalTipsReceived` absent | Treated as $0 |
| `allocatedTipsW2Box8` absent/zero | No allocated-tips override; plain unreported used |
| `nonCashTipsFmv` absent/zero | No noncash income added |
| `line5Under20PerMonthTips` absent/zero | No sub-$20 reduction; full line 6 subject to Medicare |
| `filing-status` not saved | `isMfs = false`; spouse tips included if available |
| All sources absent | `form1040.income.tipIncome = null`; no Form 4137 |

---

## Reference Data

| Constant | Value (2025) | Source | Used by |
|---|---|---|---|
| `SOCIAL_SECURITY_WAGE_BASE` | $176,100 | `ReferenceData.SOCIAL_SECURITY_WAGE_BASE` | Form 4137 line 7 |
| SS tax rate | 6.2% (literal `0.062`) | Hardcoded | Form 4137 line 11 |
| Regular Medicare rate | 1.45% (literal `0.0145`) | Hardcoded | Form 4137 line 12 |
| Additional Medicare rate | 0.9% | Form 8959 path | NOT used by line 1c directly |
| Additional Medicare thresholds | $250k MFJ / $200k S,HOH,QSS / $125k MFS | IRC §3101(b)(2) | Form 8959 |

Line 1c uses NO tax-year-specific monetary constants directly. The SS wage base only feeds Form 4137.

---

## Compute Order

1. **`computeTips(tipsTaxpayer, tipsSpouse, w2Entries, isMfs, flags, you, spouse)`** — orchestrator with MFS guard; calls `computeTipsForPerson` per spouse.
2. **`computeTipsForPerson(tipsData, w2Entries, flags, label, ssn)`** — applies `hasUnreportedTips` soft gate; runs auto-fill via `buildTipEntriesFromW2(...)`; computes per-employer accumulations.
3. **`sumW2SocialSecurityWagesAndTipsForSsn(w2Entries, ssn)`** — primary source for Form 4137 line 8.
4. **`buildIncome()`** → `income.tipIncome = roundMoney(taxpayer.line1c + spouse.line1c)` only when non-null.
5. **Schedule 2 line 5 wiring** — aggregates `taxpayer.form4137.line13 + spouse.form4137.line13`.
6. **Form 8959 Part I base augmentation** — `totalMedicareWages` includes `form4137.line6` per spouse.

---

## Cross-line Consistency

- Line 1c feeds `form1040.income.line1z` (lines 1a–1i aggregate via addNonNull), which feeds Form 1040 line 9 → AGI.
- **Noncash tips are income only.** They add to line 1c but are NOT included in Form 4137 lines 2–12 (no FICA on noncash per IRS design).
- **Under-$20 tips are income only on Form 4137.** Line 5 removes them from line 6 (Medicare tax base) while line 4 (and thus line 1c) retains the full amount.
- **Form 4137 line 6 feeds Form 8959.** Do NOT double-count by including both Form 4137 line 12 and Form 8959's portion — they cover different rates (1.45% vs 0.9%).
- **W-2 box 12 codes A and B** (uncollected SS/Medicare on tips already reported) → Schedule 2 line 13 via the `uncollected-ss-medicare-{taxpayer,spouse}` form (Form 8919 path), NOT through this computation.
- **RRTA employees do not use Form 4137 for railroad retirement taxes.** `rRTACompensationW2Box14` only contributes to Form 4137 line 8 SS wage base (to prevent over-counting); actual RRTA tax is handled by employer.
- **MFS guard (since 2026-05-06):** `computeTips(...)` returns an empty `TipResult` for the spouse when `isMfs=true`. Single guard cascades to four downstream effects: line 1c, `form4137Spouse`, Schedule 2 line 5, Form 8959 Part I base. UI also disables the spouse tip-income form on non-MFJ as primary defense.
- **Cross-line invariant:** `line 1c − Σ noncashTipsFmv == Form 4137 line 4` (when Form 4137 created).

---

## Downstream Consumers

| Consumer | How it uses line 1c |
|---|---|
| Form 1040 line 1z | One of 9 components (1a–1i) |
| Form 1040 line 9, AGI | Via line 1z |
| Pub 915 SS Worksheet 1 line 3 | Via line 1z; same MFS guard |
| Form 2441 earned income | Tip income is earned income for §21 |
| EIC earned income (line 27a) | Wages-side aggregation |
| Schedule 8812 / ACTC | Via line 1z $2,500 floor |
| Schedule 1-A line 38 (tips deduction) | Tips income contributes to the $25,000 cap |

---

## Deferred / Not Yet Wired

None. Line 1c and Form 4137 are fully implemented.

Missing test scenarios (not implementation gaps — code paths exist):
- MFJ separate Form 4137 per spouse (E2E)
- W-2 box 12 codes A/B routing to Schedule 2 line 13 (E2E)
- RRTA scenario verification (E2E)
- Timely-reported tips already in W-2 box 1 do not flow to line 1c (E2E)
